title: Phase 1: Cut the brain over to the pgvector document substrate (rip out graphiti/FalkorDB)
summary: On a dedicated worktree, rip out graphiti-core + FalkorDB and rebuild the brain service on a pgvector document substrate: a manual Notion-URL ingest endpoint (whole-page chunk -> Voyage -> store) plus recall/profile/map reads, validated on a single page before any sync or migration work.
outOfScope: Real/scheduled Notion sync, incremental last_edited_time, the migrator, multi-page ingest (a single send-a-URL endpoint only)
Section-splitting (the whole page is stored as one chunk; heading-based splitting is a later phase)
Freeform text capture and the PWA capture screen (removed with graphiti; PWA capture left cleanly disabled)
Migrating existing FalkorDB graph data (the brain restarts empty and is re-populated by re-ingesting sources)
Supabase, multi-scope assembly, per-source vs merged dedup, VPS deploy
technicalConsiderations: All work on a dedicated worktree/branch (e.g. feat/document-substrate-cutover); graphiti/FalkorDB are removed FIRST, then pgvector is built in the cleared space - no coexistence of the two substrates and no endpoints alongside graphiti at any point

Postgres + pgvector (pgvector/pgvector:pg16) replaces the FalkorDB compose service; the brain connects via asyncpg; a migration creates sources + chunks with HNSW + GIN + path-prefix + (source_id,position) indexes; vector(N) dim must match the Voyage model (voyage-3-lite)
The new pgvector recall/profile/map REPLACE the graphiti-backed reads (same FastAPI service, no /pg/* namespacing); the MCP face is repointed onto them so Claude Code keeps reading the brain
Embedder stays Voyage (reuse the existing key/config), decoupled from graphiti; no decompose/extraction LLM - splitting is string work and Phase 1 stores the whole page as one chunk
Notion fetch uses an official Notion integration token (NOTION_TOKEN); the page must be shared with the integration; path is derived from the page's ancestor chain
group_id multi-tenant isolation is dropped for now (the model uses path/scope); keep the bearer/Caddy auth shell; local dev runs Postgres in Docker with the endpoint unauthenticated
Validation is a gate: ingest the real Target-role page and confirm recall/profile/map behave before Phase 1 is considered done
